g.text: Type | Bits | Signed | Overflow Check | Formula
R_X86_64_PC32 | 32 | Yes | INT32_MIN..MAX | S + A - P

ul.li: S (Symbol): Final virtual address of the target symbol
A (Addend): Constant adjustment from relocation entry
P (Patch Site): Virtual address where relocation is applied
RIP: Instruction pointer register (points to next instruction during execution)
Used for: function pointers, vtables, global refs in data
Used for: legacy 32-bit code, small model data refs
Used for: medium code model, absolute addressing in code

g.text: call/jmp to function | R_X86_64_PC32 | RIP-relative, no base needed
Function pointer in .data | R_X86_64_64 | Full 64-bit address
Global var ref in code | R_X86_64_PC32 | RIP-relative load/store
Vtable entry | R_X86_64_64 | Full pointer needed
Small model abs addr | R_X86_64_32S | Code <2GB, sign-extend OK

text.tspan: // E8 XX XX XX XX (5 bytes)
// E8 XX XX XX XX (5 bytes)

div.p: If (sym + addend) > 0xFFFFFFFF → linker must report error
If (sym + addend) outside -2147483648..2147483647 → linker must report error

S = 0x401020 (helper address) A = -4 (addend: RIP points to next instr) P = 0x401005 (patch site address) Result = S + A - P = 0x401020 + (-4) - 0x401005 = 0x401020 - 0x401009 = 0x17 (stored little-endian as 17 00 00 00) Verify: RIP after call = 0x401009 RIP + 0x17 = 0x401020 ✓
